codeReview.summary: The feature correctly removes all Vercel dependencies (@vercel/analytics, @vercel/speed-insights) from package.json and app/layout.tsx, installs @opennextjs/cloudflare and wrangler, creates properly configured open-next.config.ts and wrangler.jsonc, adds Cloudflare Web Analytics beacon to layout.tsx, updates package.json scripts with preview/deploy commands, and updates .gitignore. All 10 fulfilled assertions (VAL-BUILD-001 through VAL-BUILD-004, VAL-VERCEL-001 through VAL-VERCEL-004, VAL-ANALYTICS-001, VAL-ANALYTICS-002) are verified as satisfied. The build succeeds with 13 pages generated. No @vercel references remain in source code.

issues.description: wrangler (^4.83.0) is listed under 'dependencies' instead of 'devDependencies'. Wrangler is a CLI/build tool and should be a dev dependency. This doesn't affect functionality but increases production dependency footprint.

The Cloudflare Web Analytics <Script> tag is placed inside <head> with strategy='afterInteractive'. Next.js's afterInteractive strategy actually injects scripts into <body>, not <head>. The visual placement in the source is misleading but the beacon will still load and work correctly. Consider using strategy='beforeInteractive' or a plain <script> tag if <head> placement is important.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The feature description specified wrangler should be installed as a regular dependency (npm install), but wrangler is a build/CLI tool that should be in devDependencies. AGENTS.md doesn't specify whether build tools should go in dependencies or devDependencies.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: package.json line 68: "wrangler": "^4.83.0" is under dependencies, not devDependencies. The feature description instruction was 'npm install --legacy-peer-deps @opennextjs/cloudflare wrangler' which defaults to dependencies.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The wrangler.jsonc env var comments were added by a later feature (verify-build-and-update-docs, commit d1c0765), not by this feature. This feature created wrangler.jsonc without the comments. This is correct per feature boundaries — noted for completeness.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: The wrangler.jsonc in commit f88f287 has 11 lines (no comments). Current wrangler.jsonc has env var comments added by commit d1c0765.

summary: PASS. The feature successfully removes all Vercel dependencies, adds the Cloudflare adapter with correct configuration, adds Cloudflare Web Analytics, updates package.json scripts and .gitignore, and the build succeeds. Two non-blocking issues found: (1) wrangler should be in devDependencies rather than dependencies, (2) the Script tag placement in <head> with afterInteractive strategy is misleading since Next.js will inject it in <body>. Neither issue blocks functionality or deployment.
